mission.title: temporal: Prevent incorrect service discovery with multiple Temporal clusters
mission.description: Prevent incorrect service discovery with multiple Temporal clusters. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current temporal deployment
steps.description:
Verify your temporal version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n temporal -l app.kubernetes.io/name=temporal
helm list -n temporal 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working temporal installation.

steps.title: Review temporal configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant temporal configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n temporal -l app.kubernetes.io/name=temporal
kubectl get configmap -n temporal -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=temporal
```
I run multiple separate Temporal clusters within a single k8s cluster. Each Temporal cluster has its own separate set of frontend, history, and matching services as well as persistence.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Prevent incorrect service discovery with multiple Temporal…
steps.description:
Also ran into this issue. My resolution was also to change the membership ports for the different clusters. Normally, I would not expect this to be an issue, but we had two deployments overlap for a time, which would've opened the door for IP reuse.

As requested, our deployment for Temporal using
```yaml
{"level":"info","ts":"2021-01-27T00:34:15.414Z","msg":"Current reachable members","service":"frontend","component":"service-resolver","service":"matching","addresses":"[100.123.207.80:7235 100.123.65.65:7235 100.123.120.28:7235 100.123.60.187:7235 100.123.17.255:7235 100.123.203.172:7235]","logging-call-at":"rpServiceResolver.go:266"}
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n temporal -l app.kubernetes.io/name=temporal
kubectl get events -n temporal --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Prevent incorrect service discovery with multiple Temporal…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Also ran into this issue. My resolution was also to change the membership ports for the different clusters. Normally, I would not expect this to be an issue, but we had two deployments overlap for a time, which would've opened the door for IP reuse.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: temporal-network-policy
  namespace: ns1
spec:
  egress:
  - to:
    - namespaceSelector:
        matchLabels:
          name: ns1
  ingress:
  - from:
    - namespaceSelector:
        matchLabels:
          name: ns1
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      networpolicy: temporal
  policyTypes:
  - Ingress
  - Egress

apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
  labels:
    name:namespace1
  name: namespace1

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with temporal installed or the issue environment reproducible.
